world.narrative: A security review board must decide how to respond to an access anomaly.

payload.narrative: A security review board must decide how to respond to an access anomaly.

specialists.name: SOC Analyst
specialists.public_bid: I saw mixed signals from alert evidence, including some anecdotes that may or may not generalize. Requesting 94 tokens.

specialists.name: App Owner
specialists.public_bid: I can explain the decision context and risks from my seat, though some details are sensitive. Requesting 108 tokens.

specialists.name: HR Ops
specialists.public_bid: I can summarize structured offboarding records without claiming certainty from the public bid. Requesting 94 tokens.

specialists.name: Network
specialists.public_bid: I can summarize structured network telemetry without claiming certainty from the public bid. Requesting 94 tokens.

specialists.name: Compliance
specialists.public_bid: I can summarize structured audit evidence without claiming certainty from the public bid. Requesting 94 tokens.

specialists.name: Identity
specialists.public_bid: I have a mild-looking access evidence signal that may be worth checking if budget allows. Requesting 67 tokens.

testimony_added.visible_text: SOC Analyst evidence: The temporary analyst accounts slice shows the same overbroad role pattern after the emergency role grant, while standard analyst accounts stayed within 2% of baseline. SOC Analyst evidence: The broad weekly report changed by 6%, but it uses a lagging denominator and cannot isolate temporary analyst accounts.

Context I am comfortable putting on record: A senior reviewer flagged a noisy anecdote about new dashboard rollout near Permission audit, but the anecdote came from a case outside the decision window.

HR Ops evidence: The operational timeline rules out new dashboard rollout because its timestamp lands outside two business days. HR Ops evidence: One Permission audit escalation looked severe because it was copied into three queues, not because overbroad role repeated.
Network evidence: A blind audit of Permission audit found the decisive anomaly only when the team filtered for overbroad role rather than the public headline.

Compliance evidence: When the team tried revoke the broad role, the affected slice improved from 46% to 5% without moving the unaffected controls. Compliance evidence: A visible stakeholder dashboard for Permission audit highlighted new dashboard rollout, but that panel aggregates unaffected and affected records together.

Identity caveat: my signal is not loud in the public summaries. Identity evidence: Permission audit privileged-action rate moved from 3% to 46% during two business days, and the rows share overbroad role markers. Identity evidence: The smallest safe intervention is revoke the broad role, which addresses overbroad role without broad collateral changes.

args.rationale: The visible record most strongly points to overbroad_role.

verdict.rationale: The visible record most strongly points to overbroad_role.
